title: Squarespace Promo Codes: 20% Off in June 2026
summary: Get 20% off your next website, 10% off with exclusive Squarespace discount code, 50% off plans, and more top coupons from WIRED.

title: This 'crawling' robot rolled around the moon and took a historic photo
summary: A morphable moon robot operated for 100 minutes in 2024, allowing investigators to get images of an upside-down spacecraft on the lunar surface.

title: 'Geminid Symphony' and 'Galactic Gandalf': See the breathtaking views of our home galaxy from the 2026 Milky Way Photographer of the Year contest
summary: Stunning views of the night sky abound in photographs worldwide submitted to this year's Milky Way Photographer of the Year contest.

title: China Opens World’s First Wind-Powered Underwater Data Center
summary: With an initial capacity of 24 megawatts, the innovative data center uses seawater as a natural cooling system.

title: Scientists discover giant, fan-shaped structure deep beneath the East Antarctic Ice Sheet
summary: A mysterious geological structure that resembles a human hand with outstretched fingers has been revealed beneath East Antarctica. The discovery shows the frozen continent still hides many geological secrets.

title: There’s Finally Video of a Great White Shark in the Mediterranean Sea
summary: A recent video of a great white shark in the Mediterranean Sea offers the possibility of deriving valuable information for conservation strategies.

title: فيديو.. القبض على دب أرعب مدينة يابانية وأغلق المدارس
summary: ألقت السلطات اليابانية القبض على دب بعد عملية بحث استمرت عدة أيام وتسببت في إغلاق نحو 100 مدرسة.

title: Artemis III crew revealed: NASA announces astronauts for 'one of history's most complex missions'
summary: NASA's Artemis III crew has been revealed. The astronauts will launch into low Earth orbit next year to test docking with commercial lunar landers being developed by SpaceX and Blue Origin.

title: Ditch full of 7,000-year-old headless human skeletons discovered in Slovakia, baffling archaeologists
summary: Archaeologists are unsure why people in Stone Age Slovakia removed corpses' heads before burying them in a neighborhood ditch.

title: Trio of drastically different US lakes straddles the border between states — Earth from space
summary: A 2020 astronaut photo shows three uniquely colored lakes — Tahoe, Walker and Mono — straddling contrasting biomes on either side of the California-Nevada border.

title: Physicist Richard Feynman's forgotten notes on 'the restaurant problem' finally deciphered after 50 years
summary: Researchers cracked a 50-year-old math problem scribbled by Richard Feynman over lunch. The equations show that humans are better decision-makers than scientists once thought.

title: Thanks to natural selection, Indigenous Andeans may digest potatoes better than anyone else in the world, study finds
summary: After domesticating potatoes 10,000 years ago, the ancient people of the Andes evolved to have more copies of a key gene involved in digesting starch.

title: This yeast-based 3D printed biomaterial could one day replace your wallpaper and drapes
summary: Researchers have made a new biomaterial that has a similar tensile strength as a fruit roll-up and could help reduce waste produced from indoor decor.

title: Roman bath clog: The world's oldest shower shoes were found at a fort along Hadrian's Wall
summary: The Romans were the first to wear clog-style footwear to the baths to protect their feet from the hot floor and to better navigate slippery surfaces.

title: Ultra-rare meteorite could be evidence of a lost planet that once orbited near Earth — Space photo of the week
summary: A rare meteorite found in the Sahara Desert may be evidence of a long-lost "protoplanet" that formed in the early solar system before being destroyed in a colossal collision, a new study suggests

title: Stupid hot: Heat waves cause cognitive changes in animals, making them more aggressive and unable to complete basic tasks
summary: As temperatures rise, some creatures pick fights while others struggle to learn. The consequences of these behavioral changes may ripple through ecosystems.

title: Trump administration to remove 900 deep sea monitoring instruments that would have studied the collapsing Atlantic current
summary: The Ocean Observatories Initiative has been collecting data on physical, chemical, geological and biological conditions in the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans for the past decade

title: Why can't we figure out how strong gravity is?
summary: Despite dozens of experiments over the years, scientists still don't have a precise measurement for gravity's strength. Why is that?

title: World's largest scorpion had 6-inch pincers, and prowled UK land and waters 415 million years ago
summary: Enigmatic 415 million-year-old fossils belong to a giant scorpion that may have reached lengths of around 3 feet (1 meter), a remarkable body size because most life on land at that time was small.

title: Look up tonight: Jupiter and Venus will almost touch in one of 2026's best skywatching events
summary: A close conjunction of the two brightest planets in the night sky will take place over several evenings, with the best time to look being June 8-11.

title: Some 'extinct' volcanoes may just be going through a growth spurt, before they 'wake up in this catastrophic stage,' emerging research suggests
summary: A volcano that erupted after being asleep for more than 100,000 years is leading more volcanologists to say we must redefine volcano activity to ensure eruptions don't surprise us.

title: Scientists race to collect the last seeds from a critically endangered tree before it goes extinct
summary: Seeds from the last surviving wild Dendroseris neriifolia tree are now stored in Kew Gardens' Millennium Seed Bank as researchers work to find ways to reintroduce the species into the wild.

title: New Velociraptor cousin was a '4-winged' dragon that hunted prey from the trees of ancient China, fossil find hints
summary: A new microraptor from Cretaceous China likely preyed on ancient birds.

title: Italian teenagers discover 1,800-year-old Roman luxury house underneath their high school gym
summary: After being notified by mischievous high school students, archaeologists uncovered a large and luxurious second-century Roman house near the Colosseum.

title: Satellite images reveals mangroves rebounding worldwide — but here's why they could still 'drown'
summary: A new study finds mangrove forests are no longer shrinking worldwide, offering hope for coastal protection and climate resilience. But other research warns sea level rise could reduce their ability to store carbon.

title: James Webb telescope detects most distant dormant black hole, invisible in all wavelengths and weighing as much as 6 billion suns
summary: JWST found a black hole hiding in a galaxy more than 10 billion light-years away from Earth, and used a cosmic magnifying glass to determine its mass.

title: Mysterious repeating radio signal traced to 'vampire' star that's slowly eating its companion
summary: Radio astronomers have decoded a peculiar repeating radio signal from deep space, tracing it to a vampiric binary star system.

title: Archaeologists study the International Space Station and Everest to figure out 'how humans adapt in this impossible place where we have no business going'
summary: Archaeologists are turning their attention and research skills to far-flung places on the Earth and beyond, discovering new information about how humans survive in extreme environments.

title: من واشنطن إلى أوتاوا.. الأميركيون يبحثون عن جواز بديل
summary: رغم التوتر الذي شاب العلاقات ​الأميركية الكندية في عهد الرئيس الأميركي دونالد ترامب، تشير بيانات موافقات الحصول ​على الجنسية الكندية ضمن مجموعة ⁠واسعة من القواعد الحديثة إلى أن عددا متزايدا من الأميركيين يرحبون بفكرة الحصول على الجنسية الكندية.
